aliases.name: Second sight
aliases.meaning: Folk term, often for spontaneous precognition

aliases.name: Presentiment
aliases.meaning: A vague, often emotional precognitive sense

description.summary: Precognition is the claimed ability to perceive or gain knowledge of future events without using known sensory channels or rational inference. Mainstream science classifies it as pseudoscience, citing the absence of replicable evidence and conflict with causality.
description.full: Precognition (Latin prae-, 'before' + cognitio, 'acquiring knowledge') is the alleged paranormal ability to perceive future events directly. It is a subtype of extrasensory perception (ESP) studied historically within parapsychology, particularly through forced-choice card tests at J. B. Rhine's Duke University laboratory from the 1930s. Notable later work includes Helmut Schmidt's random-event-generator experiments in the 1960s and Daryl Bem's nine-experiment 'Feeling the Future' paper in the Journal of Personality and Social Psychology (2011, doi:10.1037/a0021524). Bem reported small effects consistent with precognition; pre-registered replications by Ritchie, Wiseman, and French (2012, PLOS ONE) and a large 2023 multi-lab pre-registered study failed to find an effect. Samuel Soal's 1940s ESP experiments, originally cited as supportive evidence, were shown by Betty Markwick (1978) to involve data manipulation. Mainstream scientific bodies and the broader psychological community treat precognition as pseudoscience: it conflicts with the causal direction of time in physics, has not produced replicable effects, and is plausibly explained by coincidence, confirmation bias, false memory, retrofitted prediction, and selection effects.

etymology.origin: Late Latin praecognitio, from prae- ('before') and cognitio ('knowing')
etymology.first_use: English usage attested from the 17th century; modern parapsychological sense from J. B. Rhine's writings in the 1930s

related_phenomena.name: Telepathy
related_phenomena.description: Mind-to-mind communication; another claimed ESP modality

related_phenomena.name: Clairvoyance
related_phenomena.description: Direct perception of distant or hidden contemporaneous events

related_phenomena.name: Retrocognition
related_phenomena.description: Claimed knowledge of past events through anomalous cognition

related_phenomena.name: Psychic phenomena
related_phenomena.description: Umbrella category for ESP and PK claims

physical.morphology: Precognition is an alleged cognitive ability rather than a physical entity; characterised by experiential and behavioural features rather than morphology.

abilities.description: Selecting one of a fixed set of targets (e.g. Zener cards, lights, lottery numbers) before the target is determined
Producing descriptions or images of a future stimulus or event in advance of selection
A non-conscious physiological response (e.g. skin conductance) to a future stimulus, claimed in some experimental designs
Subjective experience of dreaming or visualising an event before its occurrence

patterns: Often reported in dreams and hypnagogic states
Frequently associated with high-emotion events (deaths, accidents)
Tested under forced-choice and free-response laboratory protocols
Subject to extensive criticism on methodological and statistical grounds

range.description: Precognition is reported across most human cultures and has been studied principally in laboratories in the United States and Western Europe.

hotspots.name: Duke University Parapsychology Laboratory
hotspots.notes: Site of J. B. Rhine's foundational ESP and precognition experiments, including the first systematic forced-choice card studies.

hotspots.name: Cornell University (Bem laboratory)
hotspots.notes: Site of Daryl Bem's 'Feeling the Future' experiments published in 2011.

first_recorded.source: Folkloric and religious traditions of prophecy and prophetic dreams

peak_activity_periods.description: Schmidt random-event-generator era and the rise of meta-analytic ESP studies
Bem 'Feeling the Future' and the precognition replication debate, central to the broader replication crisis in psychology

origins.cultural_roots: Precognition is rooted in long traditions of prophecy, prophetic dreaming, and divination across most of the world's religions and folklore.
origins.earliest_accounts: Ancient Mesopotamian, Egyptian, Greek (Pythia at Delphi), Hebrew, Indian, and Chinese sources record claims of foreknowledge.
origins.folklore_connections: Closely tied to folk traditions of second sight in Scotland and Ireland, prophetic dreams in many religious traditions, and oracular practice.

timeline.event: Founding of the Society for Psychical Research (SPR) in London
timeline.significance: Beginning of systematic Western study of psychic phenomena including precognition

timeline.event: J. B. Rhine publishes Extra-Sensory Perception, presenting Zener-card experiments at Duke University
timeline.significance: Foundational text of laboratory parapsychology and forced-choice ESP testing

timeline.event: Samuel Soal's ESP experiments begin in London
timeline.significance: Initially cited as strong support for ESP/precognition; later shown by Markwick (1978) to involve data manipulation
timeline.source: Markwick, B. (1978), 'The Soal–Goldney Experiments with Basil Shackleton'

timeline.event: Helmut Schmidt publishes precognition / PK experiments using electronic random number generators
timeline.significance: Inaugurates an electronic-RNG era of psi testing

timeline.event: Betty Markwick demonstrates data tampering in Soal's experiments
timeline.significance: Major historical correction to the parapsychological record
timeline.source: Markwick, B. (1978), Proceedings of the SPR

timeline.event: U.S. government Stargate Project (remote viewing) terminated; AIR review concludes operational use unjustified
timeline.significance: Ends the most prominent state-funded psi-research program
timeline.source: Mumford, M. D., Rose, A. M., & Goslin, D. A. (1995), AIR Stargate review

timeline.event: Daryl Bem publishes 'Feeling the Future: Experimental Evidence for Anomalous Retroactive Influences on Cognition and Affect' in JPSP
timeline.significance: Catalysed the modern psychology replication crisis and renewed methodological reform
timeline.source: Bem, D. J. (2011), J. Personality and Social Psychology, doi:10.1037/a0021524

timeline.event: Ritchie, Wiseman, and French publish failed pre-registered replications of Bem's experiments in PLOS ONE
timeline.significance: Independent failure to replicate; widely cited in the replication-crisis literature
timeline.source: Ritchie, S. J., Wiseman, R., & French, C. C. (2012), PLOS ONE 7(3): e33423

timeline.event: Galak et al. report seven non-replications of Bem's precognition effects in JPSP
timeline.significance: Same-journal failed replications; reinforces null result
timeline.source: Galak, J., LeBoeuf, R. A., Nelson, L. D., & Simmons, J. P. (2012), JPSP

timeline.event: Large pre-registered multi-lab replication including ESP-proponent labs reports null results for precognition
timeline.significance: Most rigorous independent test to date; null

history.modern_era: Mainstream science classifies precognition as pseudoscience: there is no accepted replicable evidence and the claim conflicts with the causal arrow of time in physics. Parapsychological organisations (Parapsychological Association, Society for Psychical Research) continue to publish on alleged psi effects, and the field is closely intertwined with the post-2011 replication crisis in psychology, which Bem's paper helped catalyse.

notable.name: Rhine ESP card experiments at Duke
notable.description: J. B. Rhine and Louisa Rhine's Duke laboratory ran large-scale Zener-card experiments testing telepathy, clairvoyance, and (especially after 1938) precognition. Rhine's 1934 monograph reported above-chance scoring rates and helped establish parapsychology as a distinct field, though independent re-examinations identified inadequate randomisation and sensory leakage in many tasks.

factors: Methodological problems identified by later analysts
Inability of subjects to maintain effects under tighter controls
credibility.notes: Foundational case in the history of psi research; not regarded today as evidence for genuine precognition.

notable.name: Bem 'Feeling the Future' nine experiments

credibility_factors: Published in a top APA journal

notable.description: Daryl Bem's nine-experiment paper reported small but statistically significant effects consistent with precognition across protocols including 'precognitive detection of erotic stimuli' and 'retroactive practice'. Pre-registered independent replications (Ritchie, Wiseman & French 2012; Galak et al. 2012; 2023 multi-lab) failed to find the effects. The paper is widely cited as a catalyst for psychology's replication-crisis reforms.

credibility.notes: Best-known modern laboratory precognition study; null on replication.

sources: Ritchie, S. J., Wiseman, R., & French, C. C. (2012), PLOS ONE 7(3): e33423

documentary.name: Bem (2011) 'Feeling the Future'
documentary.description: Nine-experiment paper reporting evidence for precognition in the Journal of Personality and Social Psychology.
documentary.analysis: Most cited modern laboratory paper on precognition; effects not replicated in pre-registered studies (Ritchie et al. 2012; multi-lab 2023). Catalyst for the replication crisis in psychology.

documentary.name: Ritchie, Wiseman & French (2012) replication
documentary.description: Three pre-registered replications of Bem's retroactive-priming experiments published in PLOS ONE.
documentary.analysis: Found no evidence for precognition; faced multiple journal rejections before publication.
documentary.source: Ritchie, S. J., Wiseman, R., & French, C. C. (2012), PLOS ONE 7(3): e33423

documentary.name: Markwick analysis of Soal data
documentary.description: Statistical analysis by Betty Markwick demonstrating data manipulation in S. G. Soal's ESP experiments (1940s).
documentary.analysis: Demonstrated Soal's results to be the product of data tampering, removing one of the strongest historical pillars of laboratory ESP/precognition evidence.
documentary.source: Markwick, B. (1978), 'The Soal–Goldney Experiments with Basil Shackleton', Proceedings of the SPR

documentary.name: Stargate Project AIR review
documentary.description: American Institutes for Research evaluation of the U.S. government's remote-viewing program (Stargate).
documentary.analysis: Concluded that operational use of remote viewing was not justified and that effects, where present, were too small for practical intelligence value.
documentary.source: Mumford, Rose & Goslin (1995), AIR Stargate review

paranormal.name: Genuine precognitive psi
paranormal.description: Mind has a real, if weak, capacity to access information from future events; a class of 'anomalous cognition' to be characterised by parapsychology.

paranormal.name: Retrocausal / quantum-influence model
paranormal.description: Speculative interpretations invoke retrocausality, quantum entanglement, or block-universe time to argue precognition is compatible with physics.
evidence_cited: Interpretive analogies to delayed-choice quantum experiments

skeptical.name: Failed replication and pseudoscience classification
skeptical.description: Mainstream scientific consensus, supported by repeated failed pre-registered replications, treats precognition as a pseudoscience.

skeptical.name: Cognitive and statistical artefacts
skeptical.description: Apparent precognitive experiences are produced by coincidence, confirmation bias, hindsight bias, false memory, retrofitting predictions to events, and selection effects in spontaneous cases; lab effects by underpowered statistics, optional stopping, and questionable research practices.
evidence_cited: Cognitive psychology of memory and probability
Open Science Collaboration (2015) findings on replicability

skeptical.name: Causality conflict
skeptical.description: Effects that precede their causes contradict the causal direction of time established in physics; absent extraordinary evidence, mundane explanations are preferred.
evidence_cited: No accepted physical mechanism for retro-causal information transfer

traditions: Prophetic dream traditions in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam
Divination and oracle practices across South and East Asia
stories: The Lincoln dream of his own assassination (popular but historically contested)
Aberfan disaster (1966) precognition reports collated by John Barker
folklore.beliefs: Precognition remains widely believed in popular culture, with surveys consistently finding that a substantial fraction of the public reports having had at least one apparent precognitive experience.

literature: An Experiment with Time — J. W. Dunne (1927)
other: Recurring trope across science-fiction and horror fiction
Frequent topic in popular-science treatments of parapsychology

researchers.name: Dr. J. B. Rhine
researchers.contribution: Founder of laboratory parapsychology; large-scale forced-choice ESP and precognition experiments

researchers.name: S. G. Soal
researchers.contribution: Mid-20th-century ESP experiments; later shown to involve data tampering (Markwick 1978)

researchers.name: Dr. Helmut Schmidt
researchers.contribution: Pioneered electronic-RNG psi experiments including precognition tests

researchers.name: Dr. Daryl Bem
researchers.contribution: Author of 'Feeling the Future' (2011); central figure in modern precognition debate

researchers.name: Dr. Dean Radin
researchers.contribution: Meta-analytic syntheses of precognition and presentiment experiments

researchers.name: Dr. Richard Wiseman
researchers.contribution: Sceptical replication and analysis of precognition claims

researchers.name: Dr. Chris French
researchers.contribution: Sceptical experimental work on precognition and ESP

organizations.name: Parapsychological Association
organizations.focus: Professional society for parapsychology, affiliate of the AAAS

organizations.name: Society for Psychical Research
organizations.focus: Founded 1882; long-running journal of psychical research

research.ongoing_studies: Continuing pre-registered replication studies of presentiment and precognition; meta-analyses by parapsychology researchers; methodological work on the 2011 Bem episode within the broader replication-crisis literature.

sources.title: Feeling the Future: Experimental Evidence for Anomalous Retroactive Influences on Cognition and Affect
sources.publication: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology

sources.title: Failing the Future: Three Unsuccessful Attempts to Replicate Bem's 'Retroactive Facilitation of Recall' Effect
Correcting the Past: Failures to Replicate Psi
sources.publication: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology

sources.title: The Soal–Goldney Experiments with Basil Shackleton: New Evidence of Data Manipulation
sources.publication: Proceedings of the Society for Psychical Research

sources.title: An Evaluation of Remote Viewing: Research and Applications (Stargate Review)
The Conscious Universe: The Scientific Truth of Psychic Phenomena
Give the Null Hypothesis a Chance: Reasons to Remain Doubtful about the Existence of Psi
